Title: Function of histone H2B monoubiquitination in transcriptional regulation of auxin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant growth and development including cell division, cell differentiation, apical dominance, flowering, senescence, and tropism.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tryptophan-dependent auxin biosynthesis via L-tryptophan supply
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the beta-subunit reaction of tryptophan synthase to produce L-tryptophan from indole and L-serine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced TSB1 expression in hub1/ckrw2 lowers auxin biosynthesis and contributes to auxin-deficient root phenotypes; L-Trp rescues ckrw2-related defects.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; H2Bub1 ChIP-qPCR; mutant analysis; double-mutant analysis; L-Trp chemical complementation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots; discussed as overlapping with CKRW2 expression in apical root.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured in 7-day-old primary roots; cytokinin induces its expression in WT but not in ckrw2.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.01 or 1 μM trans-zeatin treatment; L-Trp supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TSB1 transcript levels are significantly reduced in ckrw2/hub1 roots. 2. H2Bub1 deposition in the TSB1 coding region is reduced in ckrw2, indicating direct chromatin-level regulation by HUB1-mediated H2Bub1. 3. tsb1 shows ckrw2-like auxin-deficient root curling/waving and L-Trp rescues ckrw2, supporting TSB1 as a major downstream effector linking HUB1 to auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native genomic promoter context; no transgenic overexpression promoter used for TSB1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of TSB1 function causes reduced root length and root curling/waving consistent with auxin deficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing used to clone ckrw2/HUB1; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies TSB1 as one of the two major functional downstream genes in HUB1/2-mediated regulation of auxin homeostasis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant growth and development including cell division, cell differentiation, apical dominance, flowering, senescence, and tropism.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tryptophan-dependent auxin biosynthesis via L-tryptophan supply
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Anthranilate synthesis step feeding L-tryptophan and auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream precursor-supply step; rate-limiting step of conversion from chorismate to anthranilate as described in background
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the beta subunit of anthranilate synthase complex required for conversion of chorismate to anthranilate in tryptophan biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Background states anthranilate synthase catalyzes the rate-limiting step; paper identifies WEI7 as a major downstream effector of HUB1/2-mediated auxin regulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced WEI7 expression in hub1/ckrw2 decreases L-Trp supply and auxin biosynthesis, contributing to auxin-deficient root phenotypes; L-Trp rescues ckrw2 defects.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; H2Bub1 ChIP-qPCR; mutant analysis; L-Trp chemical complementation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots; overlaps with CKRW2 function in apical root context.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured in 7-day-old primary roots; cytokinin induces its expression in WT but not in ckrw2.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0.01 or 1 μM trans-zeatin treatment; L-Trp supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WEI7 transcript levels are significantly reduced in ckrw2/hub1 roots. 2. H2Bub1 deposition in the WEI7 coding region is reduced in ckrw2, supporting direct regulation by HUB1-mediated H2Bub1. 3. wei7 mutant shows auxin-deficient root curling/waving and L-Trp rescues ckrw2, indicating WEI7 is a major functional gene linking chromatin modification to auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native genomic promoter context; no transgenic overexpression promoter used for WEI7
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of WEI7 function causes reduced root growth and root waving/curling consistent with auxin deficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing used to clone ckrw2/HUB1; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper concludes WEI7 and TSB1 are the two major functional genes in H2Bub1-mediated regulation of auxin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant growth and development including cell division, cell differentiation, apical dominance, flowering, senescence, and tropism.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: IAA or immediate precursor to IAA in the YUCCA-catalyzed IPyA route
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: YUCCA-family flavin monooxygenase functioning in the IPyA pathway to promote IAA production from tryptophan-derived intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product or immediate final precursor in the main auxin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: YUC7 expression is reduced in hub1/ckrw2 and its coding region shows reduced H2Bub1, indicating positive chromatin-level regulation of auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: trans-zeatin treatment in overall study context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUC7 transcript abundance is reduced in ckrw2/hub1 roots. 2. H2Bub1 deposition in the YUC7 coding region is reduced in ckrw2, indicating HUB1-mediated chromatin regulation. 3. yuc7 loss of function did not show obvious ckrw2-like root curling/waving under the tested conditions, so YUC7 appears to be a regulated target but not a major phenotypic contributor in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a direct H2Bub1 target, but mutant analysis suggests weaker contribution than WEI7 and TSB1.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant growth and development including cell division, cell differentiation, apical dominance, flowering, senescence, and tropism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AMI1 expression is reduced in hub1/ckrw2 and its coding region shows reduced H2Bub1, indicating positive chromatin-level regulation of auxin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: trans-zeatin treatment in overall study context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AMI1 transcript abundance is reduced in ckrw2/hub1 roots. 2. H2Bub1 deposition in the AMI1 coding region is reduced in ckrw2, supporting direct regulation by HUB1-mediated H2Bub1. 3. ami1 loss of function did not show obvious ckrw2-like root curling/waving under tested conditions, indicating a lesser individual contribution than WEI7 and TSB1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The IAM pathway is described as proposed; AMI1 was included as a direct H2Bub1-regulated target.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant growth and development including cell division, cell differentiation, apical dominance, flowering, senescence, and tropism.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BRE1-like RING-type E3 ligase involved in histone H2B monoubiquitination
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional regulation of auxin biosynthesis via chromatin modification
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Promotes histone H2B monoubiquitination in coding regions to activate transcriptional elongation of auxin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of TSB1, WEI7, YUC7, and AMI1 via H2Bub1 deposition; CK-dependent induction especially supports activation of TSB1 and WEI7
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator of auxin homeostasis; required for normal expression of several auxin biosynthetic genes and normal endogenous IAA levels
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions non-redundantly with HUB2 in a conserved heterotetrameric E3 ligase complex; recruits/acts with UBC1/2 for H2B monoubiquitination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of HUB1 causes lower endogenous IAA, reduced auxin activity, and auxin-deficient root phenotypes; exogenous auxin rescues the phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; whole-genome resequencing; allelic analysis; molecular complementation with pHUB1::YFP-HUB1; qRT-PCR; H2Bub1 immunoblot; ChIP-qPCR; mutant phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutive/wide expression; highly active in meristematic and vascular tissues of primary root, hypocotyl, stem, cotyledon, and leaves; apical root expression overlaps auxin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Same as source species in this study; induced by trans-zeatin within 6 h and increased at transcript and protein levels
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: trans-zeatin/cytokinin treatment; exogenous auxin 2,4-D; L-Trp supplementation
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: HUB1 itself mediates histone H2B monoubiquitination (H2Bub1), an active chromatin mark, on auxin biosynthetic gene bodies; cytokinin induces HUB1 expression, increasing H2Bub1 activity
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes pleiotropic defects in leaves, seeds, root hair, apical hook, cutin, petals, flowering time, root length, meristem size, cell elongation, and gravitropism.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native HUB1 promoter in pCKRW2:GUS and pHUB1::YFP-HUB1 constructs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CKRW2 was cloned as HUB1, encoding an E3 ligase essential for global H2Bub1 in Arabidopsis. 2. HUB1 positively regulates endogenous IAA levels by promoting transcription of multiple auxin biosynthetic genes, especially WEI7 and TSB1, through H2Bub1 deposition in their coding regions. 3. Cytokinin induces HUB1 expression and H2Bub1 activity, revealing an epigenetic route by which CK modulates auxin biosynthesis and homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing for mutant cloning; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ckrw2 carries a G>A nonsense mutation converting Trp91 to a stop codon in HUB1; phenotypes are rescued by pHUB1::YFP-HUB1.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant growth and development including cell division, cell differentiation, apical dominance, flowering, senescence, and tropism.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BRE1-like RING-type E3 ligase involved in histone H2B monoubiquitination
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional regulation of auxin biosynthesis via chromatin modification
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Acts with HUB1 to catalyze histone H2B monoubiquitination associated with activation of auxin biosynthetic gene transcription
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive, inferred from HUB1/2-mediated H2Bub1 mechanism summarized by the paper
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required as part of the HUB1/2 system mediating H2Bub1-based regulation of auxin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cytokinin/trans-zeatin up-regulates HUB1/2 expression according to the paper summary
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a conserved heterotetrameric complex with HUB1; acts with UBC1/2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Participates in positive regulation of auxin homeostasis through H2Bub1, though gene-specific experiments in results focus mainly on HUB1/ckrw2.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Mediator of H2B monoubiquitination (H2Bub1) on chromatin
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic based on broad developmental roles of HUB1/2 system; specific tradeoffs for HUB2 not detailed here.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HUB2 is the paralogous E3 ligase partner of HUB1 in the H2Bub1 machinery. 2. The paper concludes that HUB1/2-mediated H2Bub1 activates transcription of auxin biosynthetic genes. 3. Although direct functional dissection centers on HUB1/ckrw2, HUB2 is included as part of the causal regulatory complex controlling auxin homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper explicitly frames the mechanism as HUB1/2-mediated H2Bub1, but core mutant and complementation evidence is strongest for HUB1.
